subject: Fix build guide/script regarding python versioning
commit_message:
Fix build guide/script regarding python versioning

Specify minimal docker release in build/requirements.txt
and build/download/requirements.txt, not a fixed one.

Change interpreter for build/package.py as it's Python 3
compliant.

Change-Id: I4352aa957371ce85bdea021101ed2f5994f9eedb
Issue-ID: OOM-1979
Signed-off-by: Bartek Grzybowski <b.grzybowski@partner.samsung.com>

entries.message:
Patch Set 1:

It has been +1 verified.

Patch Set 1: Verified+1

Build Successful 

https://jenkins.onap.org/job/offline-installer-master-review/497/ : SUCCESS

Logs: https://logs.onap.org/production/vex-yul-ecomp-jenkins-1/offline-installer-master-review/497

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

I don't think the requirements should be removed as there's the option to download and build the packages on different machines

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1
> 
> I don't think the requirements should be removed as there's the option to download and build the packages on different machines

Where do "Part 1. Preparations" state this? Build guide assumes all is done on one system afaik.

Patch Set 1: -Code-Review

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> > Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1
> > 
> > I don't think the requirements should be removed as there's the option to download and build the packages on different machines
> 
> Where do "Part 1. Preparations" state this? Build guide assumes all is done on one system afaik.

You're right, but then could be only one requirements file...

Patch Set 1:

Mateusz: It was you who added separate requirements.txt file for python dependencies in addition to already existing ./build/download/requirements.txt - can i merge them or do you have some particular reason for both of them coexisting?

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> Mateusz: It was you who added separate requirements.txt file for python dependencies in addition to already existing ./build/download/requirements.txt - can i merge them or do you have some particular reason for both of them coexisting?

In my opinion both scripts are in separated folders so they should be separated requiremnets.txt files (and that is why there is a duplicated content). If some day it will be common package with sub components. that we could have one file

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> > Patch Set 1:
> > 
> > Mateusz: It was you who added separate requirements.txt file for python dependencies in addition to already existing ./build/download/requirements.txt - can i merge them or do you have some particular reason for both of them coexisting?
> 
> In my opinion both scripts are in separated folders so they should be separated requiremnets.txt files (and that is why there is a duplicated content). If some day it will be common package with sub components. that we could have one file

I would also keep both as they are separate modules. I would maybe change == to >= for docker in both requirements files to avoid issues in the future when only one of the versions is updated.

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> > Patch Set 1:
> > 
> > Mateusz: It was you who added separate requirements.txt file for python dependencies in addition to already existing ./build/download/requirements.txt - can i merge them or do you have some particular reason for both of them coexisting?
> 
> In my opinion both scripts are in separated folders so they should be separated requiremnets.txt files (and that is why there is a duplicated content). If some day it will be common package with sub components. that we could have one file

And therefore there should be mentioned all requirements even they are the same in both requirements files, IMHO
